name: Vía Verde del Rincón de la Victoria hasta Torre del Mar – circular
region_intro: Touring cycling around Rincón de la Victoria features a diverse landscape, combining coastal paths with inland routes. The region is characterized by nine kilometers of coastline, including beaches and dramatic cliffs, alongside natural lookout points. Further inland, the terrain transitions to Mediterranean vegetation and hills, offering varied elevation profiles for cyclists. This area provides a mix of flat seaside promenades and more challenging ascents into the Axarquía region. Best touring cycling routes around Rincón De La Victoria The most popular touring cycling route is Vía Verde del Rincón de la Victoria hasta Torre del Mar – circular, a 27.1 miles (43.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 46 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a greenway, offering coastal views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Lagos Beach – View of the Torre de Güi loop from La Cala del Moral, a moderate 52.7 miles (84.8 km) path. This route provides extensive coastal scenery and views of historical towers. Local touring cyclists also love the Iznate – Osborne Bull of Almayate loop from Torre de Benagalbón, a 29.7 miles (47.8 km) trail leading through inland areas with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 3 hours 0 minutes. Touring cycling around Rincón De La Victoria is defined by coastal promenades, cliff-top paths, and routes extending into the inland hills with Mediterranean vegetation. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to more challenging climbs. The routes in Rincón De La Victoria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 270 reviews. More than 2400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Rincón De La Victoria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Rincón de la Victoria?
A: Rincón de la Victoria offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 50 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 10 easy, 16 moderate, and 27 difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Rincón de la Victoria?
A: The terrain in Rincón de la Victoria is diverse, ranging from flat coastal promenades with sea views to more challenging inland routes with significant elevation gain. You'll find paths along dramatic cliffs, through old railway tunnels, and into hills with Mediterranean vegetation, especially as you venture towards the Axarquía region.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Rincón de la Victoria offers several easy routes suitable for families or those seeking a relaxed ride. The coastal areas, particularly the magnificent seaside promenade and the Rincón de la Victoria Greenway, provide pleasant, flat rides with beautiful sea views. These routes are ideal for a leisurely pace.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Rincón de la Victoria?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976048996/via-verde-del-rincon-de-la-victoria-hasta-torre-del-mar-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Rincón de la Victoria hasta Torre del Mar – circular&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route that follows a greenway, offering coastal views.

Q: What are some scenic spots or landmarks I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you can enjoy the stunning coastline with its beaches and dramatic cliffs. Notable landmarks include the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7262649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Tesoro&lt;/a&gt;, Europe's only navigable sea cave, and the 18th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4394643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa-Fuerte de Bezmiliana&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/372627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cantal promontory&lt;/a&gt; also offers natural lookout points and old tunnels with memorable views of the Málaga coast.

Q: Is there public transport access to the touring cycling routes?
A: Rincón de la Victoria is well-connected, and public transport options are generally available to reach the starting points of many routes. Local bus services often run along the coast, providing access to different sections of the cycling network. For specific route access, it's advisable to check local bus schedules.

Q: Where can I park my car when cycling in Rincón de la Victoria?
A: Parking is generally available in Rincón de la Victoria, especially near the main beaches and urban centers. Many coastal routes start from accessible points where public parking lots or street parking can be found. For inland routes, look for parking in the smaller villages or designated areas near trailheads.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Rincón de la Victoria?
A: Rincón de la Victoria boasts over 300 days of sunshine annually and pleasant temperatures, making it suitable for cycling year-round. However, the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months offer the most comfortable conditions with milder temperatures and less crowded trails, ideal for touring cyclists.

Q: Are there options for more challenging touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38192280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osborne Bull of Almayate – Rincón de la Victoria Beach loop from La Cala del Moral&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain, reaching up to 825 feet. These routes often venture inland into the hills, providing demanding climbs and rewarding views of the Mediterranean vegetation.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Rincón de la Victoria?
A: The touring cycling routes in Rincón de la Victoria are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 270 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscape, combining beautiful coastal paths with scenic inland climbs, and the well-maintained greenways that make for enjoyable rides.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, Rincón de la Victoria and its surrounding towns like La Cala del Moral and Torre de Benagalbón offer numerous cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. The seaside promenade, in particular, is lined with eateries, perfect for a break during your ride. Many establishments are accustomed to catering to cyclists.

Q: Are the touring cycling routes in Rincón de la Victoria dog-friendly?
A: While many outdoor areas in Rincón de la Victoria are welcoming, specific regulations for dogs on cycling routes can vary. Coastal promenades and greenways are generally accessible, but it's always best to keep dogs on a leash and be mindful of other users. For more remote trails, ensure your dog is well-behaved and that local rules permit their presence.

Q: What are the typical lengths of touring cycling routes in the region?
A: Touring cycling routes in Rincón de la Victoria vary significantly in length. You can find shorter, easy rides along the coast, while more challenging routes venturing inland can extend to over 50 miles. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38205310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagos Beach – View of the Torre de Güi loop from La Cala del Moral&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate path spanning 52.7 miles (84.8 km).
tips:
Via Verde along the coast, a great thing! The route also goes through impressive tunnels blasted into the rock. The tunnels are located just before the town of Rincón de la Victoria and were originally built for a train that traveled from Málaga to Vélez. Both a passenger and a freight train ran along the railway line. The train was known as La Cochinita (little pig) because it ran over a herd of pigs on its first journey. If you look at the picture of the train that was painted on the rock face, you will find this name there. The train stopped operating in 1960.

For those who usually only drive over bridges, these tunnel passages are a real experience. Between the tunnels there are wonderful views of the coast and there is a lot to discover. Very well implemented, easy to ride cycle path on a great route, a real highlight and a great experience.

The original lighthouse was built in 1864, but due to coastal erosion and other problems, it was rebuilt and relocated several times. The current lighthouse was finally built in its current position in 1976 after several moves.

The Torre del Mar lighthouse is not only a functional part of coastal navigation, but also a popular destination for a tour, especially at sunset. The lighthouse is a fine example of the combination of modern technology with the traditions of seafaring along the Spanish coast.

The Cueva del Tesoro "Treasure Cave" is one of the most important caves in Spain and the only known sea cave in Europe that can be visited. It is located east of Málaga in Rincón de la Victoria. The cave was formed by the sea, creating impressive underground galleries, tunnels and cave rooms. It impresses with its stalactites, stalagmites and mysterious underground lakes. The name "Treasure Cave" comes from legends about hidden riches of Arab rulers, in particular a fabulous treasure from the time of the Caliphate of Córdoba.

The Cueva del Tesoro is a fascinating mix of natural wonder, history and myth. It offers not only spectacular rock formations, but also a mysterious atmosphere surrounded by its legends. Open daily, times vary depending on the season.

waypoints.name: Tunnels of the Coastal Greenway (Via Verde de la Costa), Rincón de la Victoria
